desc:
Bug 1976971 - Track flows for microtasks. r=smaug,profiler-reviewers,mstange

This adds some infrastructure so that we only add these markers when the flows feature is on.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D257057

lines.line: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
* License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
* file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */
// Directly import moz-button here, otherwise, moz-button will be loaded and upgraded on DOMContentLoaded, after MegalistAlpha is first updated.
// If we are switching from EDIT to LIST mode when `sidebarHiding` is true,
// we need to hide the sidebar because we blocked it from hiding
// previously when the user was editing a password.
// TODO(Bug 1913302): snapshotId should be optional for global commands.
// Right now, we always pass 0 and overwrite when needed.
// Prevent hiding the sidebar if a password is being edited and show a
// message asking to confirm if the user wants to discard their changes.
// TODO: This should be passed to virtualized list with an explicit height.
renderListItem({ origin: displayOrigin, username, password }, index) {
// TODO: Temporary. Should be rendered by the virtualized list.

lines.line: /* Any copyright is dedicated to the Public Domain.
// Skip since we don't expose password value
`Login entry ${i}: Rendered field value for ${selector} is correct`
// Select the button within the <panel-item> element, as that is the target we want to click.
// Triggering a click event directly on the <panel-item> would fail a11y tests because
// <panel-item>  has a default role="presentation," which cannot be overridden.
info("Check correct initial login info is rendered.");
info("click on button again to conceal the password");
// Ensure there are no saved logins
"'Export Passwords' is disabled when no logins are saved."
"'Remove All Passwords' is disabled when no logins are saved."
// We need this since removing gBrowser.selectedTab (this is the tab that has about:preferences)
// without a fallback causes an error. Leaving it causes a leak when running in chaos mode.
// It seems that our testing framework is smart enough to cleanup about:blank pages.
info("Test that reopening the sidebar should have password concealed.");
info("Open sidebar and check visibility of password field");
"Test that switching panels then switching back to Passwords should have password concealed."
